name: Picacho Trail (PR-A 117)
name_original: Sendero El Picacho – circular desde Taberno (PR-A 117)
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Trail El Picacho circular from Taberno (PR-A 117) to explore geological formations and the scenic Rambla del Acei
region_intro: Hiking around Taberno offers a range of trails through its rural landscape. The region is characterized by a mix of open countryside and gentle hills, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Hikers can explore paths that traverse agricultural zones and natural areas. The network of hiking trails around Taberno provides options for different skill levels, from easier walks to more challenging routes. Best hiking trails around Taberno The most popular hiking route is Picacho Trail (PR-A 117), a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and scenic views of the local area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from Huércal-Overa, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This trail winds through the local countryside, offering a longer excursion with notable elevation gain. Local hikers also love the The ruin loop from Albox, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Hiking around Taberno is defined by its rural landscapes, gentle hills, and open countryside. The region offers a network of trails suitable for various ability levels, including easy hikes and more challenging routes. The routes in Taberno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Taberno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Taberno?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;5&lt;/strong&gt; distinct hiking trails documented around Taberno, offering a variety of experiences through the rural landscape.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Taberno?
A: Yes, Taberno offers easy routes perfect for beginners. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38252791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The ruin loop from Albox&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 6 minutes to complete, leading through pleasant rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any longer hiking routes around Taberno?
A: For those seeking a longer excursion, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10306213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almalajeo – Camino Antiguo de Almajalejo loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.4-mile (16.7 km) trail that typically takes around 4 hours 35 minutes. Another good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10306246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.7 miles (12.4 km) long.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Taberno?
A: The best time to hike in Taberno is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, conditions of winter.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Taberno?
A: Yes, several trails are suitable for families. Easy routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38252791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The ruin loop from Albox&lt;/a&gt; provide a gentle introduction to hiking for all ages, with manageable distances and elevation changes.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Taberno?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in rural areas like Taberno. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to carry water for them, particularly during warmer months.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Taberno?
A: Hiking in Taberno offers a diverse experience through rural landscapes, characterized by a mix of open countryside, gentle hills, and agricultural zones. You'll encounter paths that traverse olive groves, dry riverbeds (ramblas), and areas with native Mediterranean vegetation.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see near the trails?
A: Yes, the region around Taberno features several points of interest. You can explore natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2547310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2470738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turtle Rock&lt;/a&gt;. For cultural sights, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2580445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario del Saliente&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building nearby.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Taberno?
A: In this rural region, parking is typically available in or near the villages where trails begin, such as Taberno or Huércal-Overa. You can often find roadside parking or small public parking areas. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Taberno using public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach specific trailheads directly in Taberno can be limited due to its rural nature. It's often more convenient to travel by car. However, local bus services might connect larger towns in the area, from which you might need to arrange further transport or walk to the starting points.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Taberno?
A: The hiking routes in Taberno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural setting, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore authentic Spanish countryside away from more crowded tourist areas.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Taberno?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Taberno are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10306246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10306213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almalajeo – Camino Antiguo de Almajalejo loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options.
tips: Simple circular trail with changing landscapes and friendly terrain. It is fixed and in some points it has catenaries to delimit it.

The Rambla del Aceituno in Taberno, Almería, is a popular hiking trail that offers a journey through stunning natural landscapes. The route is of moderate difficulty and allows you to enjoy the local flora and fauna, as well as panoramic views of the Sierra de las Estancias.

It is ideal for a leisurely walk and for those looking to connect with nature.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Trail El Picacho – circular from Taberno (PR-A 117)?
faqs.answer: The trail is approximately 8.1 kilometers long and generally takes around 2 hours and 22 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Trail El Picacho suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as having a moderate difficulty level. While it's not overly strenuous, it features varied terrain including slight inclines, flagstone paths, and sections through debris and conglomerates. Hikers should have a reasonable level of fitness and wear appropriate footwear.

faqs.question: Where does the Trail El Picacho start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular route officially starts and ends at the Town Hall square in Taberno. Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the Town Hall square, making it convenient to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse, showcasing the characteristic landscapes of the Bajo Almanzora region. You'll encounter reddish conglomerates and sandstones, sections paved with flagstones, agricultural terraces, and paths through debris. A notable part of the route includes a gorge leading to the Rambla del Aceituno, characterized by large flagstones and a canyon with impressive red walls.

faqs.question: What kind of plants and natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: Along the trail, you'll find various bushes such as hastate orach, albardine, esparto grass, fennel, albaida, and mountain rue. Agricultural terraces feature olive trees, almond trees, and some vines. The Rambla del Aceituno is a highlight with its natural waterfall and lush vegetation, while the canyon walls display striking red conglomerates.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Trail El Picacho?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the El Picacho Trail itself, the historic Calvario chapel, and the Mirador de Las Morras, which offers picturesque views of Taberno. The route also passes through the scenic Rambla del Aceituno with its unique geological formations and waterfall.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and blooming flora or vibrant fall colors. Summers can be very hot in this region, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during those months.

faqs.question: Is the Trail El Picacho – circular from Taberno (PR-A 117) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with agricultural land or potential wildlife, and to ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Trail El Picacho – circular from Taberno (PR-A 117). It is a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero del Picacho and the Cerro Alto trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead in Taberno?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in the Town Hall square of Taberno, you can expect to find local amenities such as cafes or bars in the village where you can refresh yourself before or after your hike. Restroom facilities would also be available in the village.
